description: Long markdown guide (multiple headings). Forced into Tier-2 hybrid_fallback by stubbing progressive to raise. Verifies head + TOC is preserved.
payload:
# Vector Database Performance Guide

## Overview

This section covers best practices for deploying vector databases in production with high-throughput requirements. We focus on three engines: Pinecone, Weaviate, and Qdrant.

## Indexing Strategies

### HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World)

HNSW is the default indexing algorithm. Key knobs: M parameter (16-64), ef_construction (200+), ef_search (50-200). Higher M improves recall but increases memory usage by ~8 bytes per link.

### IVF (Inverted File Index)

IVF partitions the vector space into Voronoi cells. Knobs: nlist (sqrt(N) to 4*sqrt(N)), nprobe (5-20% of nlist). IVF uses less memory than HNSW but has lower recall at the same latency.

## Memory Planning

Formula: memory_gb = num_vectors * dimensions * 4 / 1e9 * overhead_factor. HNSW overhead 1.5-2.0x; IVF overhead 1.1-1.3x. Plan for 2x the current vector count.

## Production Checklist

- Set up monitoring for recall degradation
- Configure automatic index rebuilding thresholds
- Implement request-level timeout (recommend 100ms)
- Enable WAL for crash recovery
- Set memory limits to 80% of available RAM
